title: SACRAMENTO, CA

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The Marine Mammal Center has received an annual allocation of $2 million since 2015 to fund marine mammal rescue organizations along the California coast.",
    "The Budget Act of 2024 restored funding for the Marine Mammal Center, which was eliminated in the Governor’s May Revise budget proposal.",
    "The final phase of the Developmental Services Provider Rate Reform is on track to increase rates for providers who deliver care to individuals with developmental disabilities starting July 1, 2024.",
    "The Legislature rejected a proposal to delay rate increases for service providers, which could have disrupted care for vulnerable residents.",
    "The Active Transportation Program (ATP) is the state’s primary funding source for local projects that promote active transportation, such as walking and biking.",
    "The ATP has been critical in reducing greenhouse gas emissions and enhancing public health, with 85 percent of funds dedicated to projects in disadvantaged communities.",
    "The budget proposal cut ATP funding by $559 million, endangering various projects in the North Bay before the Legislature restored the funds.",
    "The California Nutrition Incentive Program (CNIP) encourages low-income individuals to purchase locally grown, healthy produce by providing incentives at farmers’ markets.",
    "A study by the University of California, Nutrition Policy Institute, found that the majority of CNIP participants are women, with 56 percent identifying as Latino/a.",
    "The study also showed that CNIP participants had overwhelmingly positive experiences and increased their purchases of locally grown and organic produce.",
    "The Legislature's funding proposal for CNIP is expected to incentivize the purchase of over 114 million servings of California-grown fruits and vegetables by low-income Californians and provide economic stimulus to California small farmers."
  ]
}
```
